title: juniperus horizontalis var. alpina
intro: Juniperus horizontalis var. alpina, also known as creeping juniper, is a low-growing, spreading evergreen shrub native to the mountainous regions of North America. It possesses medicinal properties, particularly its berries, which have been used in traditional medicine for their antiseptic and antifungal properties. The health benefits of creeping juniper include its ability to aid digestion, relieve urinary tract issues, and reduce inflammation, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Creeping juniper can be prepared in various forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
